question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a server-side gRPC interceptor for subscription deduplication in grpcServerConfigurer() to subscribe to the same gossip topic multiple times on the same connection, consuming all maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection() slots with duplicate streams and blocking other gossip subscriptions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the readOnly TransactionTemplate bean by triggering code paths that attempt write operations within the read-only transaction context, causing transaction rollbacks that disrupt gossip message retrieval and prevent transactions from being delivered to subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the readOnly TransactionTemplate to cause long-running read transactions that hold database read locks, preventing the gossip message writer from committing new transaction records and effectively blocking gossip propagation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the readOnly TransactionTemplate by subscribing to a gossip topic with a very old start time, causing the server to open a long-running read-only transaction that scans large amounts of historical data, consuming database connection pool resources and blocking gossip delivery for other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the readOnly TransactionTemplate by sending many concurrent gossip subscription requests that each open a read-only transaction, exhausting the database connection pool and preventing new gossip transactions from being written or read?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the readOnly TransactionTemplate by triggering a code path that incorrectly uses the readOnly transaction for a write operation, causing a database exception that propagates up and crashes the gossip subscription handler, preventing transaction delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the readOnly TransactionTemplate by crafting a gossip subscription request that causes the server to execute a query with a full table scan inside the read-only transaction, consuming all available database I/O bandwidth and preventing gossip transaction writes from completing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the readOnly TransactionTemplate by sending a gossip subscription request with a topic ID that does not exist, causing the server to repeatedly query the database inside read-only transactions and consuming database connection pool resources needed for gossip delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the readOnly TransactionTemplate by sending many concurrent gossip subscription requests that each trigger a read-only transaction, causing database connection pool exhaustion that prevents the gossip importer from writing new transaction records?
